description_long: This bollard is a tall, rectangular column with a beige body featuring subtle relief patterns that resemble clouds or abstract shapes. The structure is topped with a prominent, smooth, purple dome cap, which stands out visually against the neutral base. The upper section of the bollard has four open rectangular windows, giving it a lantern-like appearance and allowing visibility through the top portion. The surface appears to be made of molded plastic or composite material, with a matte finish and slight textural variation. The base is flat and unadorned, suggesting it is designed to stand upright on a hard surface. There are no visible reflective markings, chains, or connectors, and the overall design is simple yet functional for urban access control.
description: A tall, beige rectangular bollard with a purple domed cap and open windows near the top, featuring subtle relief patterns on its surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height, the purple dome cap, and the open window facing forward with visible relief patterns.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the depth of the windows and the continuation of the relief patterns along the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back window and the symmetrical design, with the purple dome cap clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the same window and relief pattern arrangement as the other side, emphasizing the uniformity.
